items.title: Jamie Dimon Warns Market Risk Looks A Lot Like 2005–2007 — Some Are Doing 'Dumb Things'
items.description: Today's financial environment shows similarities to the period before the 2008 financial crisis, with higher asset prices and increased risk-taking across the industry, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE:JPM) Chief Executive Jamie Dimon. Talking at the bank's annual investor day last month, Dimon said the financial industry is getting comfortable with heavy borrowing and high asset prices, a pattern that was also seen in the years before the 2008 financial crisis. items.title: Algorand CEO calls blockchain the end of TradFi
items.description: Moving from the halls of JPMorgan and the U.S. Treasury to the forefront of the digital asset world has given Staci Warden a unique perspective on money. items.title: Warsh to meet Tillis as Senate confirmation remains blocked
items.description: Sen. Thom Tillis, R-N.C., has refused to advance Warsh's confirmation until a probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell is dropped.
